reasoning: Dan Osborn formed an exploratory committee on April 3, 2025, to challenge Senator Pete Ricketts in the 2026 election. This was followed by the release of a poll on April 11 showing him in a statistical tie (46-45) with the incumbent. Such significant moves—the committee formation and the release of favorable polling—strongly suggest a high-momentum entry into the race. Historically, Osborn announced his 2024 candidacy in September 2023, approximately 13-14 months before the election. For the 2026 cycle, he is already active as of April 2025 (19 months out), suggesting an accelerated timeline to capitalize on his 2024 name recognition and the 'mechanic vs billionaire' populist framing. While he previously experienced a delay between 'mulling' a run in November 2024 and forming the committee in April 2025, the formal step of committee formation typically leads to a candidacy announcement within a 1-3 month window. June 1st provides an 8-week window from the committee's launch. Given the favorable polling and early start, a late spring announcement is more likely than not.
